title: Northstar event gateway: architecture decisions
intro: Northstar is a fictional event-ingestion service for retail telemetry. This board captures the decisions needed to move from a single-region prototype to a production launch without hiding operational tradeoffs.

submit.label: Submit architecture decisions
submit.note: Selections become the implementation brief for the Northstar delivery team.

blocks.md:
## Decision context

Northstar accepts telemetry bursts from 4,000 retail locations.

Peak traffic is twelve times the hourly average.

Producers retry for up to fifteen minutes after a network outage.

Consumers need ordered events within each store, not across stores.

The current prototype runs in one region and shares its database with reporting.

The launch target is 120,000 events per second with a 99.95% ingest SLO.

The team has six engineers and one embedded SRE.

Choose the smallest architecture that preserves a credible path to regional failover.

blocks.title: Current request path
blocks.source:
graph LR
  Device --> Gateway
  Gateway --> API
  API --> Database
  Database --> Reporter

blocks.prompt: Which service boundary should Northstar launch with?

options.label: Modular monolith with an internal queue
options.hint: One deployable, explicit ingest and delivery modules

pros: Module seam supports a later split
cons: A process crash interrupts both ingest and delivery

visual.title: Modular monolith
visual.source:
graph LR
  Client --> API
  API --> Queue
  Queue --> Worker
  Worker --> Store

options.label: Separate ingest and delivery services
options.hint: Independent deploys joined by a durable broker

visual.title: Split services
visual.source:
graph LR
  Client --> Ingest
  Ingest --> Broker
  Broker --> Delivery
  Delivery --> Store
  Delivery --> Audit

visual.title: Edge routing sketch

blocks.prompt: What consistency contract should the delivery API expose?
Which capabilities belong in the launch slice?

options.label: Per-tenant quotas
options.hint: Prevents one retailer from starving others

options.label: Historical backfill
options.hint: Useful after launch, expensive in the hot path

blocks.title: Degraded-mode policy
blocks.summary: Choose the overload behavior and explicitly approve its customer impact.

children.prompt: How should ingest behave above the regional safety limit?
Approve a customer-visible degraded mode for overload events?

detail.md: Approval permits the API to return an explicit degraded response instead of accepting work it cannot deliver inside the SLO.

blocks.title: Proposed retry and overload policy
blocks.diff:
diff --git a/config/service.yaml b/config/service.yaml
index 76ad1b2..a8f4ca1 100644
--- a/config/service.yaml
+++ b/config/service.yaml
@@ -8,7 +8,12 @@ ingest:
-  max_inflight: 25000
-  retry_after: 1s
+  max_inflight: 40000
+  retry_after_min: 250ms
+  retry_after_max: 8s
+  retry_jitter: 0.25
+  shed_at_percent: 92
+  recover_at_percent: 78
   request_timeout: 3s
@@ -24,4 +29,8 @@ delivery:
-  attempts: 5
+  attempts: 8
+  backoff: exponential
+  dead_letter_after: 15m
+  preserve_store_order: true
+  max_batch: 500
diff --git a/internal/routing/policy.go b/internal/routing/policy.go
index c4a1170..8a7d031 100644
--- a/internal/routing/policy.go
+++ b/internal/routing/policy.go
@@ -17,8 +17,15 @@ func (p Policy) Route(event Event) Target {
-	return p.primary
+	if p.primary.Healthy() {
+		return p.primary
+	}
+	return p.secondary.ForStore(event.StoreID)
 }
 
-func (p Policy) Retry() time.Duration {
-	return time.Second
+func (p Policy) Retry(attempt int) time.Duration {
+	delay := min(250*time.Millisecond<<attempt, 8*time.Second)
+	return withJitter(delay, 0.25)
 }
@@ -39,3 +46,9 @@ func (p Policy) Accept(load float64) bool {
-	return load < 1
+	if load >= 0.92 {
+		return false
+	}
+	if load <= 0.78 {
+		return true
+	}
+	return p.wasAccepting
 }
diff --git a/deploy/alerts.yaml b/deploy/alerts.yaml
index 6bdcc02..aff1339 100644
--- a/deploy/alerts.yaml
+++ b/deploy/alerts.yaml
@@ -11,6 +11,18 @@ groups:
       expr: rate(ingest_errors_total[5m]) > 0.01
       for: 10m
+    - alert: IngestSheddingActive
+      expr: rate(ingest_shed_total[2m]) > 0
+      for: 2m
+      labels:
+        severity: page
+      annotations:
+        runbook: northstar/ingest-shedding
+    - alert: DeadLetterLagHigh
+      expr: dead_letter_oldest_seconds > 300
+      for: 5m
+      labels:
+        severity: ticket
diff --git a/docs/runbook.md b/docs/runbook.md
index fa89c44..cc98382 100644
--- a/docs/runbook.md
+++ b/docs/runbook.md
@@ -3,3 +3,12 @@
 1. Confirm producer error rate.
 2. Check regional storage latency.
+3. Compare inflight load with the 92% shedding threshold.
+4. If the primary is unhealthy, verify store routing in the secondary.
+5. Pause replay while dead-letter age exceeds five minutes.
+6. Notify affected tenants before forcing a regional drain.
+
+## Recovery
+
+Resume replay at 10% and double every five minutes while error rate stays below 1%.

blocks.prompt: Should the proposed retry and overload policy become the launch baseline?

options.label: Gate adoption on a 2× load test

blocks.prompt: Where should the durable event log live?
Which producer-facing API should launch first?
What is the minimum launch observability stack?
How should retail locations authenticate producers?
What should happen when one event repeatedly fails delivery?

options.label: Quarantine and continue
options.hint: Preserves throughput with an audit gap

blocks.prompt: Which regional topology should the first production release use?
Which launch SLO should the team publish?

options.label: 99.9% ingest during pilot
options.hint: More error budget, weaker customer promise

blocks.prompt: Approve the selected Northstar architecture as the production implementation brief?

pros: Thirteen explicit decisions with visible tradeoffs
Launch scope and degraded behavior are reviewable
cons: Capacity numbers still require load-test confirmation
